title: Mycelium Canvas Common Definitions
description: Shared $defs for canvas YAML schemas. Provenance, trace edges, confidence enums, ISO timestamps.

evidence_type.description: Gilad's evidence ladder. Match the level of evidence to the claim — never overclaim.

confidence.description: Either a float 0.0-1.0 (Gilad confidence meter) or one of the evidence_type enum values.

source_class.description: Classification of an evidence source's origin. external_human = real conversation with a real person (interview, usability test, survey response). external_data = third-party analytics, behavioral data, market data. internal_desk = desk research, competitive analysis, brainstorming. internal_simulated = mocked persona interview, thought experiment.

provenance.description: Per-entry provenance for high-stakes canvas claims. Documents the evidence behind each claim so canvas-as-source-of-truth doesn't drift toward confident speculation.

evidence_sources.description: Identifiers for the evidence: interview IDs, research brief refs, external URLs, behavioral data sources, etc.

source_classes.description: Parallel array to evidence_sources classifying each source's origin. Optional — unclassified sources default to internal_desk for ratio calculations. Added in v0.11.0 to address the internal/external evidence ratio Goodhart trap.

trace_edge.description: A directed edge in the canvas trace graph. Mirrors BDSK's 10 canonical edge types.

target_id.description: Identifier of the linked artifact (canvas section, decision log entry, eval scenario, etc.)

edge.description: Canonical edge type. Borrowed from BDSK's trace.upstream model (Daniel Bentes feedback).

trace.description: Bidirectional trace links for an artifact. Optional for most canvas entries; required for high-stakes entries.

schema_version.description: Canvas schema version. Increment on breaking changes (additive changes do not require a bump).
